summary: DHH announces the third Building of Basecamp workshop, to be held in San Francisco on November 9th, 2004. The workshop is a collaboration with Adaptive Path, who will run a 'Building Blogger' workshop the day before. Attendees can purchase both days for $745 or a single day for $395. DHH also mentions arriving early and offers to meet up with San Francisco locals to discuss Ruby on Rails or Instiki.
key_points: 37signals is running its third Basecamp workshop, indicating growing interest in their product and methods
Partnership with Adaptive Path signals 37signals' positioning within the web design and UX community
DHH's open invitation to discuss Ruby on Rails shows the early grassroots community-building around the framework
money_quotes: if there's any SF natives interested in meeting up to talk about Ruby on Rails, Instiki, or anything else, I'm around
key_insight: This brief 2004 announcement captures the early days of both Basecamp workshops and Ruby on Rails community-building, with DHH casually inviting meetups around a framework that would soon reshape web development.
